ops.text: ccx code grep's default (index) engine returned "0 matches" repo-wide for `contentdStamp` — a symbol with 16 real occurrences across internal/cli/contentd.go and contentd_lifecycle_test.go (the file had just been edited this session). Re-running the same query with --regex (rg engine) found all 16. A silently stale index that reports confident zero matches is worse than an error: it invites "doesn't exist" conclusions from a shallow search. The index engine should detect dirty/recently-modified files and either reindex or fall through to the rg engine instead of reporting 0.
